summary: High‑quality audience tape of a solid late‑80s show with standout versions of Peggy‑O, Sugar Magnolia and To Lay Me Down.
review: The recording is an audience source that many reviewers describe as full, clear and even “hot,” capturing the band’s tight mid‑80s performance. Highlights include a crisp Jerry vocal on “To Lay Me Down,” a soaring “Sugar Magnolia,” and a powerful rendition of “Peggy‑O.” The second set benefits from a quiet, attentive crowd that adds intimacy without detracting from the sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently positive remarks about both musical performance and audio fidelity across five detailed reviews; show quality weighted heavily, recording quality rated excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Full and clear sound with a warm tape character; occasional instrument imbalance due to stage‑side mic placement noted by one reviewer, minor tape hiss mentioned for the soundboard reference only.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows smoothly, with a especially sweet second‑set atmosphere thanks to a quiet, attentive audience; the pre‑drum list adds an interesting touch.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Clear vocals praised on “To Lay Me Down” and strong guitar work noted on “Sugar Magnolia.”
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drum list highlighted as interesting, no negative comments about the drumming.
